check: No PII in event names or properties
description: Audit every event and property for emails, names, free-text user input, and tokens. Use opaque user IDs; mask session-replay inputs by default.
why_it_matters: PII in analytics tools creates shadow copies of personal data outside your deletion/retention controls

description: Verify with the browser devtools: before consent no analytics requests fire (or a documented cookieless basis applies); after denial nothing loads; consent changes propagate to all tools including replay and ads pixels.
why_it_matters: A banner that doesn't control the scripts behind it is compliance theater — and a KVKK/GDPR finding

description: Set explicit retention windows in each tool (GA4 defaults to 2 months for some tables), and include analytics tools in the user-deletion runbook.
why_it_matters: Deletion requests legally extend to analytics vendors; defaults are rarely what you want

check: Single source of truth per metric
description: Every reported metric (signups, MRR, conversion) has one canonical definition and query; dashboards reference it rather than re-deriving.
why_it_matters: Two dashboards disagreeing on signups costs more trust than having no dashboard

check: Revenue events reconciled against the payment provider
description: Weekly automated or manual check: analytics `order_completed` count/sum vs Stripe/Shopify records within a tolerance (e.g. 2%).
why_it_matters: Silent event loss is invisible until someone bets a decision on it

description: Enable bot filtering, exclude office/VPN IPs and staging domains, and tag internal users so they're excludable in queries.
why_it_matters: Team traffic and bots can dominate small-site stats and flatter every metric

description: When the same action can arrive from client and server (e.g. purchase), dedupe by a shared event/insert ID — mirrors the Ads module's pixel+CAPI dedup rule.
why_it_matters: Double-counted conversions inflate results and misallocate ad spend

description: A documented UTM convention (lowercase, fixed source/medium vocabulary), campaign links built from a shared sheet/tool, and first-touch UTMs persisted onto the user at signup.
why_it_matters: Inconsistent UTMs fragment channels into dozens of unjoinable variants

check: Dashboards have owners and review cadence
description: Each dashboard names an owner and a cadence (weekly growth review, monthly board pack); orphaned dashboards get deleted.
why_it_matters: Unowned dashboards rot into wrong numbers that someone eventually quotes

description: The track plan lists every event: name, trigger, properties, added-by, added-on — generated from or verified against code.
why_it_matters: Six months later nobody remembers what `flow_completed_v2` means or why it double-fires on Safari

description: An automated alert when core event volume drops >50% day-over-day or hits zero — the analytics equivalent of uptime monitoring.
why_it_matters: Instrumentation breaks silently on redesigns; you find out during the board meeting otherwise

description: Pre-register the metric and sample size (or use a sequential method); no stopping at first significance; log every experiment and its outcome, including losers.
why_it_matters: Peeking and cherry-picking make A/B testing an expensive random-number generator
